raw_summary: A nearly five-minute total lunar eclipse is occurring in western North America, starting at 3:16 a.m. Pacific Daylight Time and lasting until 4:58 a.m. Pacific Time. It is the shortest total lunar eclipse of the century, with parts of South America, India, China, and Russia also able to see the eclipse. The effect, known as the "blood moon," is caused by the Earth's atmosphere filtering out most of the blue light. NASA's Mitzi Adams will answer questions about the eclipse on Twitter.

Keith Wolfe Smarch, a First Nation master-carver, had abandoned work on a totem pole to guide me along the infamous Chilkoot Trail, where hundreds of men and thousands of pack animals lost their lives as they headed to Canada's frozen wastes during the Klondike Gold Rush.
Nearby I glanced down on a harness smothered in lichen lying beside the skull of a mule, while strewn all around me were the remains of rusty cans and oil drums.
A leather boot, white with mould, lay half-buried at my feet.
My favourite was the Tagish Wilderness Lodge - a speedboat whisks you across a lake on a 45-minute journey from the nearest road.
The discovery started a stampede along the treacherous mountain track through snowdrifts, blizzards and avalanches, each man weighed down with supplies.
Clapping hands to warn of our presence, we scrambled along a rocky path, stepping over tangled roots and clambering over fallen logs.
I felt as if I was treading on hallowed ground.
At Tombstone Territorial Park we pulled in to watch caribou grazing on the tundra and beavers paddling to their lodge, mouths crammed with twigs.

raw_summary: A Cleveland police officer, Michael Brelo, is facing voluntary manslaughter charges for shooting and killing unarmed suspects Timothy Russell and Malissa Williams while standing on the hood of their car, during a high-speed chase in 2012. Brelo, the only officer charged out of the 13 who fired their weapons that night, is accused of waiting until after the initial volley of shots to reload and discharge the last of his 49 rounds, which struck the suspects multiple times when they were no longer a threat. Prosecutors argue that Brelo acted out of rage and vengeance, and violated departmental policies, while defense attorneys maintain that he had reason to believe he was being shot at. A judge will decide Brelo's guilt or innocence, and he faces a maximum sentence of 25 years in prison if convicted. Footprints on the hood of the suspects' car matched those found on a police cruiser that Brelo had climbed on top of to fire, and experts have determined that another officer fired three of the shots during Brelo's volley. The incident spurred a US Justice Department probe, revealing a pattern of excessive force by Cleveland police officers, and the city has already paid $3 million to the families of Russell and Williams in a lawsuit settlement.

Those allied with Hadi have accused the Iranian government of supporting the Houthis in their uprising in Yemen.
The Houthis forced President Abdu Rabu Mansour Hadi from power in January, though Hadi still claims he is Yemen's legitimate leader and is working with the Saudis and other allies to return to Yemen.
The resolution "raises the cost" for the Houthis, according to Mark Lyall Grant, Britain's ambassador to the United Nations.

Also Tuesday, Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi and Saudi Arabian Minister of Defense Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz agreed to form a joint military commission to discuss the implementation of a major strategic maneuver inside Saudi Arabia, Egypt's state-run Ahram news agency reported.
They include Yemen's current ambassador to the United Nations, Khaled Mahfoodh Abdulla Bahah, who said Tuesday, "We refuse (the) influence of Iran in Yemen affairs."

In addition to the arms embargo, it also demands that the Shiite group pull back and refrain from more violence and includes sanctions aimed at controlling the spread of terrorism, according to Grant.
In response to media reports, Egyptian officials said no troops have yet been sent to Yemen.

The neon yellow socks or 'volt' in Nike parlance are aimed to draw attention to the bright blue cleats.

But striker Alex Morgan hailed Nike's design as innovative.
'It's the first time as well that (the kit) is fully integrated, so going from the boot right to the sock, then it fades from the volt into the white kit,' Lotti said, 'It's a real head-to-toe look.'

Kilmarnock interim manager Gary Locke has been given the role on a permanent basis after signing a three-year deal.
The 39-year-old - who will speak at a press conference on Friday morning - has lost just once in seven games since taking over at Rugby Park.
hallucinations.regenerated: The former Hearts assistant manager, Gary Locke, took over as interim manager at Kilmarnock in February following Allan Johnston's resignation. He has since signed a three-year deal and will address the media on Friday. (Rewritten)

raw_summary: Eric Cantona, Manchester United legend, claimed his old side are "kings of the city" again after their derby win and predicted they can win the Premier League next year under Louis van Gaal. Cantona believes United's focus on developing young players in their academy gives them an edge over rivals City in the long run.
final_summary: Eric Cantona, Manchester United legend, claimed his old side are "kings of the city" again after their derby win and predicted they can win the Premier League next year under Louis van Gaal. Cantona believes United's focus on developing young players in their academy gives them an edge over rivals City in the long run.

raw_summary: A mother-of-twins, Hannah Moore, had her Instagram account deleted after posting pictures of her post-pregnancy stretchmarks to boost her confidence and inspire others. The images were deemed inappropriate and contained nudity and violence, leaving Hannah feeling disgusted and judged. Instagram admitted it was a technical mistake and reinstated her account.
